diagrams.title: Unified Process Phase Overview
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the four UP phases with their time distribution, key activities, and primary artifacts. The phases are iterative, with each phase potentially containing multiple iterations.
elements: Inception Phase (10% time) - Vision Document, Use-Case Model v0.1, Risk List, Project Plan
Elaboration Phase (20% time) - SAD, Refined Use Cases, Prototype, Architecture Baseline
Construction Phase (50% time) - Implementation Model, Test Cases, Incremental Releases
Transition Phase (20% time) - Deployed System, User Manuals, Training Materials
diagrams.purpose: Visualize phase progression and deliverables in UP lifecycle

diagrams.title: UP Iteration Structure
diagrams.description: A spiral diagram showing how each iteration covers all workflows: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Test → Deployment, with emphasis shifting based on phase.
elements: Iteration N: Requirements (10%), Design (15%), Implementation (50%), Test (25%)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate iterative nature of UP and workflow emphasis variation

diagrams.title: UP vs Waterfall Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing sequential nature of Waterfall versus iterative-incremental nature of UP with overlapping phases.
elements: Waterfall: Requirements → Design → Implementation → Test → Deploy (sequential, no overlap)
UP: Multiple iterations per phase, each producing increments; phases overlap slightly; continuous feedback loop
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate fundamental differences between sequential and iterative models
